title: Inside the High-Stakes Legal Battle Between Elon Musk and OpenAI
subtitle: A personal diary and claims of broken promises take center stage in a trial that could reshape the future of artificial intelligence.

summary.en: Elon Musk is currently suing OpenAI and its leaders, Sam Altman and Greg Brockman, over claims that the company abandoned its original non-profit mission. The trial has focused heavily on private diary entries written by Brockman during the company's early years. Musk argues that the founders tricked him and turned the firm into a for-profit business for personal gain. OpenAI denies these accusations, calling the lawsuit a jealous attempt to hurt a competitor. The case continues to draw attention as more witnesses, including high-profile tech leaders, prepare to testify.
summary.zh: 伊隆·馬斯克目前正對 OpenAI 及其領導人山姆·奧特曼與葛瑞格·布羅克曼提起訴訟，指控該公司背棄了最初的非營利使命。審判重點大量集中在布羅克曼於公司草創時期所寫的私人日記內容。馬斯克主張創辦人欺騙了他，並為了個人利益將公司轉為營利事業。OpenAI 否認這些指控，稱該訴訟是出於嫉妒而試圖傷害競爭對手的手段。隨著包括多位科技界重量級人物在內的證人準備出庭作證，此案持續受到高度關注。

paragraphs.en: A major legal battle is currently unfolding in court, pitting billionaire Elon Musk against the artificial intelligence company he helped start, OpenAI. The trial, which is now in its second week, centers on Musk’s claim that OpenAI’s leaders, CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman, betrayed the company’s founding mission. According to The Verge, Musk argues that the firm was created to develop AI for the benefit of humanity, but later shifted its focus toward making money. Musk is now seeking the removal of Altman and Brockman from their roles and is asking for up to $150 billion in damages to be given to the company’s non-profit organization.
paragraphs.zh: 一場重大的法律戰目前正在法庭上演，億萬富翁伊隆·馬斯克（Elon Musk）與他共同創辦的人工智慧公司 OpenAI 對簿公堂。這場審判已進入第二週，核心爭議在於馬斯克指控 OpenAI 的領導層，即執行長山姆·奧特曼（Sam Altman）與總裁葛瑞格·布羅克曼（Greg Brockman），背棄了公司的創立宗旨。根據科技媒體 The Verge 報導，馬斯克主張該公司成立的初衷是為了人類福祉開發人工智慧，但後來卻將重心轉向營利。馬斯克目前正尋求撤換奧特曼與布羅克曼的職務，並要求賠償高達一千五百億美元，這筆款項將捐給該公司的非營利組織。

paragraphs.en: Much of the recent focus in the courtroom has been on Greg Brockman. As reported by The Guardian, Musk’s legal team has used Brockman’s personal diary, written during the company’s early years around 2015, to argue that the founders were dishonest. Musk’s lawyers have read several private entries aloud to the jury, suggesting that Brockman and Altman planned to turn the company into a for-profit business while hiding their true intentions from Musk. In one entry, Brockman wrote about the desire to make billions and questioned how he could reach that financial goal. Another entry mentioned that it would be "morally bankrupt" to take the non-profit away from Musk.
paragraphs.zh: 近期法庭審理的焦點多集中在布羅克曼身上。據英國《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，馬斯克的法律團隊引用了布羅克曼在二〇一五年左右公司草創時期所寫的私人日記，試圖證明創辦人言行不一。馬斯克的律師團在陪審團面前朗讀了數則私人日記內容，暗示布羅克曼與奧特曼當時就計畫將公司轉為營利事業，並對馬斯克隱瞞了真實意圖。在其中一則日記中，布羅克曼寫下了賺取數十億美元的渴望，並質疑自己該如何達成此一財務目標。另一則日記則提到，將非營利組織從馬斯克手中奪走在道德上是「破產的」。

paragraphs.en: Brockman has spent several days on the witness stand explaining these notes. According to Ars Technica, he described the experience as "very painful." He told the court that the diary was never meant to be read by the public. He explained that the entries were a "stream of consciousness"—a way of writing down his thoughts as they came to him—rather than a formal record of his actions. He testified that he often wrote down different viewpoints or ideas from others to help him think through difficult decisions. Brockman insisted that he and Altman were always honest with Musk, and he criticized Musk for taking his private words out of context to make them look bad.
paragraphs.zh: 布羅克曼在證人席上花了好幾天解釋這些筆記。根據科技網站 Ars Technica 報導，他形容這段經歷「非常痛苦」。他向法庭表示，這些日記從未打算公開。他解釋說，這些內容只是「意識流」的紀錄——即隨手寫下當下的想法——而非正式的行動紀錄。他作證指出，他經常寫下他人的不同觀點或想法，以協助自己釐清困難的決策。布羅克曼堅稱他與奧特曼對馬斯克始終誠實，並批評馬斯克將他的私人言論斷章取義，藉此抹黑他們。

paragraphs.en: OpenAI has strongly rejected all of Musk’s claims. The company argues that Musk is simply an "aggrieved former co-founder" who left the business in 2018 after he failed to take control of the company. In a blog post published earlier this year, OpenAI stated that the lawsuit is a "baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor." The company maintains that it is still overseen by a non-profit organization that remains committed to its original goal of helping humanity through AI. They also suggest that Musk’s own companies, such as xAI, are competing with OpenAI, which is why he is trying to cause trouble for them in court.
paragraphs.zh: OpenAI 強烈否認馬斯克的所有指控。該公司主張，馬斯克不過是一位「心懷不滿的前共同創辦人」，在二〇一八年因無法奪取公司控制權而離職。OpenAI 在今年稍早發布的部落格文章中指出，這場訴訟是「毫無根據且出於嫉妒，意圖阻礙競爭對手發展的手段」。公司堅稱，目前仍由一家非營利組織監督，並持續致力於透過人工智慧造福人類的初衷。他們也暗示，馬斯克旗下的公司（如 xAI）正與 OpenAI 競爭，這才是他試圖在法庭上製造麻煩的原因。

paragraphs.en: The trial has brought many important figures into the spotlight. The Verge reports that the court has already heard from Musk’s financial manager, Jared Birchall, and former OpenAI board member Shivon Zilis. The list of people scheduled to testify is long and includes some of the most powerful names in the technology industry. For example, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is expected to appear, as is former OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever. The testimony of these leaders could provide more clarity on how OpenAI and Microsoft worked together, especially regarding the development of their AI models.
paragraphs.zh: 這場審判讓許多重要人物成為焦點。The Verge 報導稱，法庭已聽取了馬斯克的財務經理傑瑞德·伯查爾（Jared Birchall）以及 OpenAI 前董事會成員希馮·齊里斯（Shivon Zilis）的證詞。預定出庭作證的名單很長，其中包括科技產業中最具影響力的一些名字。例如，微軟（Microsoft）執行長薩提亞·納德拉（Satya Nadella）預計將會出庭，OpenAI 前首席科學家伊利亞·蘇茨克維（Ilya Sutskever）亦然。這些領導者的證詞可能有助於釐清 OpenAI 與微軟之間的合作模式，特別是在人工智慧模型開發方面的細節。

paragraphs.en: As the trial continues, the court is also examining important documents that were previously kept secret. One such document is a 2019 contract between Microsoft and OpenAI, which defines "artificial general intelligence"—a term for a highly advanced system that can perform most valuable work better than humans—as a key goal. This contract and other evidence are helping the judge, Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers, and the jury understand the complex history of the company.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著審判持續進行，法庭也正在檢視先前列為機密的重要文件。其中一份文件是微軟與 OpenAI 在二〇一九年簽署的合約，該合約將「人工通用智慧」（Artificial General Intelligence）定義為關鍵目標，這指的是一種高度先進、能在大多數有價值的工作上表現優於人類的系統。這份合約與其他證據正協助法官伊芳·岡薩雷斯·羅傑斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers）及陪審團理解該公司複雜的發展歷程。

paragraphs.en: While the legal battle is far from over, the impact on the tech world is already clear. The case has forced a public look into the private decisions of OpenAI’s founders and the rapid growth of the AI industry. With closing arguments expected soon, the final decision will likely have a major influence on how AI companies are managed and how they balance the goal of making money with the goal of serving the public. For now, both sides remain firm in their positions, and the courtroom remains the center of a high-stakes fight over the future of technology.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這場法律戰遠未結束，但對科技界的影響已顯而易見。此案迫使大眾得以一窺 OpenAI 創辦人的私人決策過程以及人工智慧產業的快速成長。隨著結辯即將到來，最終判決很可能會對人工智慧公司的管理方式，以及如何在營利與服務公眾的目標之間取得平衡產生重大影響。目前，雙方仍堅持各自立場，法庭依然是這場關於科技未來、高風險爭鬥的核心戰場。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To set someone or something in conflict or competition against another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（坑洞），這裡作動詞，指兩方對立。文中：A major legal battle is currently unfolding in court, pitting billionaire Elon Musk against the artificial intelligence company he helped start, OpenAI.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prevent a process or plan from succeeding.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指火車出軌，這裡引申為破壞計畫。文中：In a blog post published earlier this year, OpenAI stated that the lawsuit is a "baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor."
原意指舞台上的聚光燈，這裡指受到大眾矚目。文中：The trial has brought many important figures into the spotlight.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Strongly felt and unlikely to change.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（公司），這裡作形容詞，表示立場堅定。文中：For now, both sides remain firm in their positions, and the courtroom remains the center of a high-stakes fight over the future of technology.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: OpenAI president’s ‘deeply personal’ diary becomes focus in Musk’s case against Altman

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: OpenAI president forced to read his personal diary entries to jury

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI

quiz.question: What specific action is Elon Musk requesting from the court regarding the leadership of OpenAI?

options.A: He wants the court to force OpenAI to merge with his own company, xAI.
options.B: He is seeking the removal of Sam Altman and Greg Brockman from their positions.
options.C: He is demanding that the company be dissolved and its assets sold to the public.
options.D: He wants the court to appoint him as the new CEO of the non-profit organization.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段明確指出，Musk 正在尋求將 Altman 和 Brockman 從他們的職位上撤職（removal of Altman and Brockman from their roles）。選項 A 提到 xAI 是競爭對手，但並非 Musk 在訴訟中的要求；選項 C 錯誤，因為 Musk 是要求賠償金給非營利組織而非解散公司；選項 D 並未在文中提及。

quiz.question: Based on the text, what can be inferred about the relationship between Greg Brockman’s diary entries and his defense in court?

options.A: Brockman admits that the diary was a formal record of his business intentions.
options.B: Brockman believes the diary entries prove he and Altman were always honest with Musk.
options.C: Brockman argues that the diary contains private, unrefined thoughts that do not represent his actual business conduct.
options.D: Brockman claims that the diary was written by someone else to frame him.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段，Brockman 解釋日記是「意識流」（stream of consciousness），即隨筆紀錄想法，而非正式的行動紀錄，並批評 Musk 將私人筆記斷章取義。這暗示他認為日記內容不能代表他實際的商業行為。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 雖然他堅稱誠實，但重點在於他如何解釋日記的性質；選項 D 文中完全未提及。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'derail' mean as used in the context of OpenAI's statement?

options.A: To cause a train to go off its tracks.
options.B: To obstruct or hinder the progress of something.
options.C: To provide financial support for a new project.
options.D: To change the legal structure of a company.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到 OpenAI 稱此訴訟是為了「derail a competitor」，意指 Musk 試圖阻礙競爭對手（OpenAI）的發展。雖然 A 是該詞的字面義，但在這裡是用於比喻；C 與原意相反；D 並非該詞的含義。在商業語境中，derail 通常指破壞或阻撓計畫的順利進行。

quiz.question: What is the central focus of the article regarding the legal battle between Musk and OpenAI?

options.A: A technical analysis of how OpenAI’s artificial intelligence models function.
options.B: A biography of the early years of Greg Brockman and Sam Altman.
options.C: A detailed report on the financial success of Microsoft’s investments in AI.
options.D: The conflict over OpenAI’s shift from its original non-profit mission to a profit-driven model.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章開頭即點出這場法律戰的核心是 Musk 指控 OpenAI 背棄了為人類利益開發 AI 的初衷，轉而追求獲利。全文圍繞著這個核心衝突展開，包括 Musk 的指控、OpenAI 的辯解、法庭上的證據（日記）以及此案對科技界的影響。選項 A、B、C 皆僅為文章中的部分細節，而非整體主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
